text.title: Product Explainability (PM Angle) Core Rules
text.summary: PM-side communication discipline producing one canonical feature narrative (problem -> behaviour change -> outcome) and audience-specific renders for execs / sales / support / customers.

rule.statement: Each feature has ONE canonical narrative artefact owned by the PM: {problem, who_has_it, behaviour_change_we_predict, outcome_metric, evidence_link}.
rule.rationale: Multiple competing narratives confuse customers; one canonical source eliminates drift.

rule.statement: Audience-specific renders MUST derive from the canonical narrative — never authored independently. Re-renders flag back to PM for canonical update.
rule.rationale: Independent renders drift; derived renders keep one source of truth.

rule.statement: Each narrative MUST link to >=1 evidence artefact (customer quote, metric movement, research session) supporting the predicted outcome. 'We believe' without evidence is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Evidence-free narratives collapse under exec questioning.

rule.statement: The canonical narrative MUST pass the 90-second test: a non-product person reads it and can repeat the problem + behaviour change.
rule.rationale: Narratives requiring more than 90s to land fail in real exec conversations.

rule.statement: The outcome line MUST be a customer-state change ('users now publish in <2 min'), not a feature shipped ('we added a publish button').
rule.rationale: Feature-as-outcome obscures whether the work actually moved customers.

rule.statement: If applicability conditions fail (any Skip-If trigger holds — infra-only release; GTM owns canonical narrative; internal-tool product; pre-launch with no behaviour change yet), the agent MUST skip this methodology and route to the named alternative or to the methodology fallback.
rule.rationale: Mis-applying a methodology outside its preconditions degrades output quality and pollutes downstream artefacts; the explicit skip-rule keeps the routing tree honest.
